2605.12154.title: MM-OptBench: A Solver-Grounded Benchmark for Multimodal Optimization Modeling
2605.12154.paper_summary: Optimization modeling translates real decision-making problems into mathematical optimization models and solver-executable implementations. Although language models are increasingly used to generate optimization formulations and solver code, existing benchmarks are almost entirely text-only. This omits many optimization-modeling tasks that arise in operational practice, where requirements are described in text but instance information is conveyed through visual artifacts such as tables, graphs, maps, schedules, and dashboards. We introduce multimodal optimization modeling, a benchmark setting in which models must construct both a mathematical formulation and executable solver code from a text-and-visual problem specification. To evaluate this setting, we develop a solver-grounded framework that generates structured optimization instances, verifies each with an exact solver, and builds both the model-facing inputs and hidden reference files from the same verified source. We instantiate the framework as MM-OptBench, a benchmark of 780 solver-verified instances spanning 6 optimization families, 26 subcategories, and 3 structural difficulty levels. We evaluate 9 multimodal large language models (MLLMs), including 6 frontier general-purpose models and 3 math-specialized models, with aggregate, family-level, difficulty-level, and failure-mode analyses. The results show that the task remains far from solved: the best two models reach 52.1% and 51.3% pass@1, while on average across the six general-purpose MLLMs, pass@1 is 43.4% on easy instances and 15.9% on hard instances. All three math-specialized MLLMs solve 0/780 instances. Failure attribution shows that errors arise both when extracting instance data from text and visuals and when turning extracted data into solver-correct formulations and code. MM-OptBench provides a testbed for solver-grounded, decision-oriented multimodal intelligence.
2605.12154.paper_summary_zh: 優化建模將現實決策問題轉化為數學優化模型和可執行的求解器實現。雖然語言模型越來越多地用於生成優化公式和求解器代碼，但現有的基準幾乎完全是文本形式的。這忽略了許多在操作實踐中出現的優化建模任務，這些任務的要求以文本描述，但實例信息則通過表格、圖形、地圖、時間表和儀表板等視覺材料傳達。我們引入了多模態優化建模，這是一個基準設定，其中模型必須從文本和視覺問題規範中構建數學公式和可執行的求解器代碼。為了評估這一設定，我們開發了一個基於求解器的框架，該框架生成結構化的優化實例，使用精確的求解器驗證每個實例，並從相同的經過驗證的源構建模型面向的輸入和隱藏的參考文件。我們將該框架具體化為 MM-OptBench，這是一個包含 780 個經過求解器驗證的實例的基準，涵蓋 6 個優化家族、26 個子類別和 3 個結構難度級別。我們評估了 9 個多模態大型語言模型 (MLLMs)，包括 6 個前沿通用模型和 3 個數學專用模型，並進行了總體、家族級、難度級和失敗模式分析。結果顯示，這項任務仍然遠未解決：最佳的兩個模型達到 52.1% 和 51.3% 的 pass@1，而在六個通用 MLLMs 中，容易實例的 pass@1 平均為 43.4%，而困難實例則為 15.9%。所有三個數學專用 MLLMs 解決 0/780 個實例。失敗歸因顯示，錯誤既發生在從文本和視覺中提取實例數據時，也發生在將提取的數據轉化為求解器正確的公式和代碼時。MM-OptBench 為基於求解器的、以決策為導向的多模態智能提供了一個測試平台。
2605.12154.author: Zhong Li et.al.
2605.12154.authors: Zhong Li, Qi Huang, Yuxuan Zhu, Mohammad Mohammadi Amiri, Niki van Stein, Thomas Bäck, Matthijs van Leeuwen, Zaiwen Wen, Lincen Yang
